title: staff — field classification
description: Staff carries three tiers of sensitivity: public employment metadata (position, department, is_branch_manager), internal operational data (employment_type, status, started_at, weekly_hours, availability), and two locked-away clusters — CONFIDENTIAL (emergency contact + tax_id_last4, gated by `staff.view.emergency_contact`) and REGULATED_FINANCIAL (compensation columns, gated by `staff.view.compensation`, 7-year retention in the dedicated staff_compensation_history table). Coach carries mostly public / internal data (bio + rating + primary sport visible on the tenant's public directory); certifications are internal-tier unless verified via HR (then they become public-tier when the coach_certifications_verified entitlement is on). The redactor pipeline applies the field-level gates on egress — a caller with `staff.view` but WITHOUT `staff.view.compensation` sees a Staff object with compensation_* keys entirely stripped from the JSON.

public: Staff.employment_type (surface value; the exact wage/salary is confidential)
internal: Coach.certifications (structural shape — visible; verified state gated by coach_certifications_verified when on)
Coach.public_email (when unset — coach hasn't published a public address)
confidential: Staff.termination_reason (HR-visible — knowing WHY a colleague left is HR-only unless the caller carries staff.view.compensation.own on their own row)
Coach.public_email (when set — the coach opted to publish an address; still confidential until the coach's user surface renders it)
restricted: Staff.termination_reason (when termination_reason='terminated' — disciplinary. Always internal even to HR peers outside the tenant's compliance officer role.)
regulated_financial: staff_compensation_history rows (dedicated audit table with 7-year retention; every mutation to a compensation_* field writes here)
audit_log: Staff.compensation_type / compensation_amount_cents / compensation_currency / pay_frequency (every mutation → staff_compensation_history table with 7-year retention; NOT the normal audits table)
Staff.emergency_contact_* + tax_id_last4 (every mutation → normal audits table, 7-year retention for confidential-tier fields)

notes: The redactor pipeline (from telemetry::RedactorProcessor) applies to every Staff row on egress. Callers with `staff.view.compensation` see compensation_*; callers with `staff.view.compensation.own` see their OWN compensation_* only. Callers with neither see the fields stripped from the JSON entirely.
The redactor pipeline (from telemetry::RedactorProcessor) applies the same pattern for emergency contact + tax_id_last4 — gated by `staff.view.emergency_contact` (or `.own`).

Staff.notes and Coach.notes are HR-visible internal fields — they do NOT surface on the tenant activity feed. When the notes contain compensation discussions or disciplinary reasoning, they inherit the confidential tier of the enclosing context.

Compensation writes DO NOT feed the activity feed. HR peers should not accidentally see 'X changed Y's salary' scrolling past — the CompensationChanged event routes to the compensation-audit queue exclusively.

Tax_id_last4 is the LAST 4 digits only. The full tax ID (SSN, national tax ID) is never stored — a full-ID storage would put us in PCI/HR-regulated-data scope we deliberately avoid. Payroll integration (Wave 4 Finance) forwards to the 3rd-party payroll processor which owns the full-ID storage responsibility.

GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure) on offboarded staff: emergency_contact_* + tax_id_last4 are HARD-PURGED on ended_at + 90 days (`user.privacy.erasure_hold_days` default 30 is short-form; staff module uses 90 days for the employment-record grace window). Employment metadata (position, dates, termination_reason) retains 7 years for legal record.

regulated_financial rows in staff_compensation_history retain 7 years even after the parent Staff row is hard-deleted. The retention pipeline keeps the audit rows alive per financial regulation. This mirrors facility::retention.json for booking financial records.
